pattern: Consonants never move, so the problem reduces to deciding one output order for just the vowel letters (by frequency, ties by first appearance) and then pouring that sequence back into the original vowel slots left to right.
hints: Consonants stay exactly where they are; only the vowel characters get rearranged among themselves, so first separate the two concerns: which vowel goes where, and where the vowel slots even are.

Count how many times each distinct vowel appears and note the position where each first appears; sort the distinct vowels by frequency descending, and a stable sort on frequency alone will naturally keep first-appearance order for any ties.

Expand the sorted distinct vowels back into a full sequence (each vowel repeated by its frequency) and drop that sequence, in order, into the string's vowel positions from left to right; every other character is copied through unchanged.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a mix of vowels with different frequencies
statement example, every vowel appears exactly once so nothing moves
no vowels at all, the string must come back unchanged
vowels in reverse alphabetical order, each appearing once: ties must break by position, never by letter order
two vowels tied on frequency but interleaved, checking the tie resolves by whichever vowel's letter first appeared earliest
consonants interleaved with a frequency tie among vowels, checking consonant positions stay fixed

stress.description: random lowercase strings up to the full length constraint
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: vowel-heavy random strings to stress the frequency grouping and tie-breaking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    vowels = 'aeiou'
    consonants = 'bcdfg'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(vowels if rng.random() < 0.7 else consonants) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
